## DEFECT 3 — Phantom unsaved draft in Clarifications (clear root cause + fix)

### Symptom

On a tender's Overview tab, the Clarifications & Communications panel shows:

> 📝 You have an unsaved draft from just now. Restore draft / Discard

…without the user ever clicking "+ Add entry" or typing into the clarification
form. The draft persists across page reloads (it's in IndexedDB via
`FormDraftStore`) and the timestamp ticks forward as expected.

### Root cause

`apps/web/src/pages/tendering/TenderClarificationLog.tsx` (around lines
126-151) calls `useFormDraft({...})` but does **not** pass `isDirty`.

In `apps/web/src/drafts/useFormDraft.ts:140-163`, the `visibilitychange`
auto-save listener fires every time the page goes hidden (tab switch,
in-page navigation, app backgrounded). Line 145:

```ts
const dirty = isDirtyRef.current ? isDirtyRef.current() : true;
if (!dirty) return;
```

When `isDirty` isn't provided, `dirty` defaults to **true**. So the auto-save
fires unconditionally, persisting the **empty** form values to IndexedDB.

After that initial empty save, `FormDraftStore.get(userId,
"tender_clarification_entry_create")` always returns a row, `DraftBanner`
renders, and the user sees a "draft from just now" they never created.

### Fix

Pass `isDirty` to the `useFormDraft` call. The predicate should require both:

1. the form is actually open (`adding === true`), and
2. at least one user-content field is non-empty.

Example (adapt to the existing variables in `TenderClarificationLog.tsx`):

```ts
const draft = useFormDraft({
  formType: draftFormType,
  contextKey: tenderId,
  schemaVersion: 1,
  getValues: () => ({ entryKind, direction, noteType, subject, text, dueDate, occurredAt }),
  setValues: (d) => { /* unchanged */ },
  isDirty: () =>
    adding &&
    (subject.trim() !== "" ||
     text.trim() !== "" ||
     dueDate !== "" ||
     occurredAt !== "")
});
```

`entryKind`, `direction`, and `noteType` are intentionally excluded from the
`isDirty` check — those default to non-empty enum values and would otherwise
make every open form count as dirty even with nothing typed.

### Audit the other consumers

Grep for `useFormDraft` and check each consumer for the same omission. From a
prior grep there are at least these call sites:

- `apps/web/src/pages/workers/AvailabilitySection.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/TenderClarificationLog.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/pages/forms/FormFillPage.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/pages/field/FieldSafetyPage.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/contacts/ContactsTab.tsx`

For each, either pass an `isDirty` that returns false on an untouched form, or
explicitly comment why "save on any visibilitychange" is the intended
behaviour. The phantom-draft class of bug will recur in any panel that loads
on the Tender Detail (or similar) page without an `isDirty` guard.

If a consumer can't easily detect "touched", the alternative (smaller scope
change) is to also harden `useFormDraft.ts` so the default behaviour is
`isDirty=false` when not provided — fail closed instead of open. That's a
behavioural change to a shared hook, so prefer the per-consumer fix; flip the
default only if multiple consumers need it.

### Tests

- Unit test the new `isDirty` predicate behaviour in
  `TenderClarificationLog`: open the form, type, close, switch away; check the
  draft save fires only when expected. (May need lightweight harness mocking
  `visibilitychange`.)
- Manual smoke: load a tender's Overview, switch tabs away and back without
  ever opening Clarifications → no DraftBanner should appear.

---

## DEFECT 2 — Activity timeline Post (verification needed)

### Symptom as reported

The smoke test reported: typing into the Activity timeline "Add a note…"
input on a tender's Overview tab and clicking **Post** resulted in a timeline
entry showing `"Quick edit by Alex Admin: notes"` (an auto-log from a prior
scope-notes edit), NOT the typed content. The typed text appeared to be
silently discarded.

### Likely explanation

The reporter (Cowork) drove the input via the Claude in Chrome `form_input`
tool, which sets `input.value` but may not always dispatch the React-internal
`change` event that updates the controlled-input state (`newNote`).
`postNote()` in `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/TenderDetailPage.tsx` (line
366-382) has:

```ts
const postNote = async () => {
  if (!tender || !newNote.trim()) return;
  ...
};
```

If `newNote` stayed empty (because `setNewNote` was never called by React),
`postNote` returns early — no POST, no new note. The "Quick edit by Alex
Admin: notes" entry the user saw was the unrelated audit-log entry from a
prior scope-notes edit, **not** a corruption of the typed content.

In other words: this is plausibly a testing artifact, not a real product bug.

### What to do

Verify by **manual** test, not by automation:

1. Open any tender's Overview tab in the running dev app.
2. Click into the Activity timeline "Add a note…" input.
3. Type a clear, unique string (e.g. `MANUAL-TEST <ISO-timestamp>`).
4. Click **Post**.
5. Confirm the timeline now shows a new entry with the typed string as its
   body, alongside any prior entries (including auto-logs).

If it works → Defect 2 was a testing artifact. Document the verification
result in the PR body and close Defect 2 with no code change.

If it does NOT work — typed text disappears for real users typing in the
input — trace why `newNote` doesn't reflect the typed value. Likely
candidates: the input's `value` binding, the `onChange` handler, a stale
closure in `postNote`, or a refresh-on-blur that clears state before submit.
Fix appropriately and add a regression test.

fix only if the bug is real). Do not change unrelated tender-detail or
clarification behaviour.
